description.en: The world's oldest Chinatown, established in 1594. A chaotic, dense paradise for food lovers with hand-pulled dumplings, fried siopao, and Chinese-Filipino fusion dishes on every corner.
description.tl: Ang pinakamatandang Chinatown sa mundo, itinatag noong 1594. Paraiso ng mga mahilig sa pagkain.

tips: Start at Binondo Church and work your way through
Try hopia, lumpia, and Shanghai fried siopao

description.en: Famous for pan-fried buns with crisp bottoms and juicy pork filling. A Binondo institution that has been satisfying cravings for decades. Simple, cheap, and absolutely delicious.
description.tl: Sikat sa priniritong siopao na malutong ang ilalim at masarap ang laman. Isang institusyon ng Binondo.

tips: Pan-fried buns are the signature - crispy bottom is key

description.en: No-frills Binondo dim sum institution where you point at steaming bamboo baskets to build your meal. Siu mai, hakaw, and congee at unbeatable prices in the heart of Chinatown.
description.tl: Walang pakong dim sum institution sa Binondo kung saan ituturo mo ang pagpili mula sa mga bamboo basket.

tips: Siu mai and hakaw are the best sellers

description.en: New 24-hour restaurant by Chef Kel Zaguirre bringing a modern, playful twist to Chinatown classics. Dim sum and then some, with zany visual presentation in a vibrant setting.
description.tl: Bagong 24-oras na restoran ni Chef Kel Zaguirre na may modernong twist sa klasikong pagkain ng Chinatown.

tips: Open 24 hours - great for late night cravings
By the team behind Food Records group

description.en: Manila's trendiest nightlife district packed into a few walkable blocks. Rooftop bars, speakeasies, live music venues, and creative hole-in-the-wall restaurants draw the city's cool crowd.
description.tl: Ang pinaka-trendy na nightlife district ng Maynila sa ilang block na naglalakad.

address.tl: Poblacion, Lungsod ng Makati, Kalakhang Maynila

tips: Walk between bars - everything is close
Check out Polilya, Run Rabbit Run, and Spirits Library

description.en: Hidden above a Ministop in Poblacion, this spot reworks Filipino staples into convincing plant-based versions. Crispy bagnet, sisig, and kare-kare that impress vegans and non-vegans alike.
description.tl: Nakatago sa itaas ng Ministop sa Poblacion, ang lugar na ito ay nagre-remake ng mga Filipino staples sa plant-based na mga bersyon.

address.tl: Poblacion, Lungsod ng Makati, Kalakhang Maynila

tips: Hidden above a Ministop - look for the stairs
Great food period - not just for vegans

description.en: The historic walled city built by the Spanish in the 16th century. Cobblestone streets, Fort Santiago, San Agustin Church, and horse-drawn kalesa rides through centuries of Philippine history.
description.tl: Ang makasaysayang lunsod na may pader na itinayo ng mga Kastila noong ika-16 na siglo.

tips: San Agustin Church is a UNESCO site
Take the free Pasig River ferry to get here

name.en: Ugly Duck Tapas and Rooftop Lounge

description.en: Hidden gem rooftop bar with exceptional food including Mushroom Salpicao, Oyster Aburi Sisig, and Paella Negra. Intimate atmosphere with views over the city.
description.tl: Nakatagong rooftop bar na may pambihirang pagkain kasama ang Mushroom Salpicao at Paella Negra.

address.tl: BGC, Lungsod ng Taguig, Kalakhang Maynila

tips: Less known than the big rooftop bars

description.en: Retro compound of vintage shops, art galleries, thrift stores, and laid-back bars. Manila's creative and hipster hub where local artists, musicians, and vinyl collectors gather.
description.tl: Retro compound ng mga vintage shop, art gallery, thrift store, at mga tahimik na bar. Hub ng mga creative at hipster ng Maynila.

address.en: General Romulo Avenue, Cubao, Quezon City, Metro Manila
address.tl: General Romulo Avenue, Cubao, Lungsod Quezon, Kalakhang Maynila

tips: Fred's Revolucion bar is a local institution

description.en: Upscale weekend market in the heart of Makati's business district. Artisanal Filipino food, organic produce, specialty coffee, and pastries from small local vendors.
description.tl: Pamilihang panlinggo sa gitna ng business district ng Makati. Artisanal na pagkaing Pilipino at specialty coffee.

address.en: Jaime Velasquez Park, Salcedo Village, Makati, Metro Manila
address.tl: Jaime Velasquez Park, Salcedo Village, Makati, Kalakhang Maynila

description.en: Chef JP Anglo's playful modern Filipino restaurant in Legazpi Village. Elevated versions of classic dishes like fried lumpia stuffed with milkfish and crispy kare-kare.
description.tl: Modern Filipino restaurant ni Chef JP Anglo sa Legazpi Village. Mga elevated version ng mga klasikong putahe.

address.en: Ground Floor, LPL Mansion, Legazpi Village, Makati, Metro Manila
address.tl: Ground Floor, LPL Mansion, Legazpi Village, Makati, Kalakhang Maynila

tips: Fried lumpia with milkfish is the signature

description.en: Rooftop bar offering the best panoramic view of the Intramuros golf course and Manila sunset. Craft cocktails with a stunning backdrop of the historic walled city below.
description.tl: Rooftop bar na nag-aalok ng pinakamahusay na panoramic view ng Intramuros golf course at Manila sunset.

tips: Combine with an Intramuros walking tour

description.en: Binondo institution since 1956 famous for its crispy fried chicken and fresh lumpia. Three generations have kept the recipes unchanged. The go-to for authentic Chinese-Filipino comfort food.
description.tl: Institusyon ng Binondo mula pa noong 1956, sikat sa crispy fried chicken at sariwang lumpia.

tips: Always packed at lunch - go early

description.en: Hidden compound in Makati featuring contemporary art galleries, artisan shops, a popular cafe, and a craft pub. A quiet escape from the city's noise favored by Manila's creative class.
description.tl: Nakatagong compound sa Makati na may mga contemporary art gallery, artisan shop, at craft pub.

address.en: 2316 Chino Roces Avenue, Makati, Metro Manila
address.tl: 2316 Chino Roces Avenue, Makati, Kalakhang Maynila

tips: Easy to miss from the street - look for the gate
Excellent cafe and craft pub inside

description.en: The larger of Makati's two weekend markets. More diverse food offerings including regional Filipino specialties, organic produce, and the best bibingka and puto bumbong stalls.
description.tl: Ang mas malaking weekend market ng Makati. Mas sari-saring pagkain kasama ang regional Filipino specialties.

address.en: Legazpi Active Park, Legazpi Village, Makati, Metro Manila
address.tl: Legazpi Active Park, Legazpi Village, Makati, Kalakhang Maynila

tips: Sunday only - bigger than Salcedo Market
Arrive early for the best selection
